# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

Write secure code.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { open } from "https://deno.land/x/deno_kv@0.12.3";  // Deno KV adapter imports here... (add your deno kv version)
const db = await open("./data");   // Initialize the database connection with './data' as key-value store name in this case, you can use any other path too if needed for different databases. Add .catch block to handle errors and retry logic here... (add your error handling code). 
import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.149.0/http/server.ts"; // Import the http server module, you can use any other if needed for different servers like express or koa etc.. Add .catch block to handle errors and retry logic here... (add your error handling code).
const port = 8000;  // Set a default listening port number as per requirement. You may change it based on the requirements of project, add try catch blocks for exceptions in this part if needed......(Add exception catching block)   .listen(() => console.log("Listening at http://localhost:" + port));
// Create an HTTP server and listen to requests... (add your request handling code here).  You can use any other middleware or framework like express, koa etc for this purpose......(Add appropriate error catching block)   .catch((e: Error) => console.error("Server failed with the following exception", e));
